response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and a long career in distributed, high-throughput systems, but the role's core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is essentially absent from his profile. His C++ usage appears only in early-career roles (pre-2001) and a bachelor's thesis; his listed skills and recent 15+ years of work are firmly in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. There is no evidence of routing tile pipelines, navigation data systems, or the latency-sensitive geospatial processing the role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform built for NGOs and conservation professionals
Demonstrated distributed-systems and high-throughput experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency design
Strong TDD and software-craftsmanship culture, with evidence of quality practices across multiple roles
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; C++ appears only in pre-2001 roles and a thesis project — the primary technical requirement is unmet
GIS experience is oriented toward data collection and display for NGOs, not routing tile generation, pipeline latency reduction, or navigation accuracy
No background in navigation guidance systems, routing data pipelines, or traffic-signal integration — the domain-specific knowledge required by the role is missing
